reasoning: N,N'-bis(1,4-dimethylpentyl)-phenylenediamine is a p-phenylenediamine derivative where each nitrogen bears two alkyl substituents (one being the 1,4-dimethylpentyl group and one being the aryl ring nitrogen), making each amine a secondary alkyl-aryl amine; the compound is best described as a dialkylarylamine. The core structure is a benzene ring with two secondary amine groups substituted with branched alkyl chains.
